NOTICE OF PETITION FOR ORDER PERMITTING LATE CLAIM AGAINST GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY [GOV.C 946.6], PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER GARAY, JOSE GARAY, NANCY HEARING SET FOR AUG-25-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONERS JOSE GARAY, NANCY GARAY'S PETITION FOR ORDER PERMITTING LATE CLAIM AGAINST GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY IS GRANTED. G.C. SECTION 946.6 IS LIBERALLY CONSTRUED SO AS TO HAVE CASES HEARD ON THEIR MERITS. DRAPER V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1990) 52 CAL 3D 502. PETITIONERS DEMONSTRATED THAT BELL EXERCISED REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN INVESTIGATING PETITIONERS' CLAIM AND RESPONDENTS FAIL TO OFFER EVIDENCE OF PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORTS DIRECTOR JOHN L. MARTIN, SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: off calendar as moot. Parties stipulated to allow leave to amend. Court grants leave to amend. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Petition For Order Permitting Late Claim Against Governmental Entity [Gov.C 946.6]
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael A. Gerchow of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3936) for respondent and opposing party; Steven R. Weinmann and Hillary R. Allyn of the Arns Law Firm (495.7800) for petitioners and moving parties.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONERS JOSE GARAY, NANCY GARAY'S PETITION FOR ORDER PERMITTING LATE CLAIM AGAINST GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY is granted. G.C. section 946.6 is liberally construed so as to have cases heard on their merits. Draper v. City of Los Angeles (1990) 52 Cal 3d 502. Petitioners demonstrated that Bell exercised reasonable diligence in investigating petitioners' claim and Respondents fail to offer evidence of prejudice. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
